note: Pan-India megastar; both audiences know him, though the romantic-hero persona and DDLJ-era nostalgia are most embedded in the Hindi belt.
Huge in the Hindi heartland for mass action; the 'Bhai' cult and Eid-release ritual are far weaker in Tamil Nadu, which has its own mass icons.
His content-driven films (3 Idiots, Dangal) and the Tamil-remake/dubbing pipeline give him strong recognition in Tamil Nadu too.
Iconic across India; KBC host fame helps, but the 'Angry Young Man' baggage and Sholay-era reverence are Hindi-cinema canon.
Bollywood A-lister of the Kapoor dynasty; Animal and Sanju gave wider reach, but he is far less of a household name in Tamil Nadu.
High-energy Bollywood star; recognizable via big films and ads, but his quirky North-Indian-pop persona doesn't carry the same weight in TN.
Top Bollywood heroine; RRR cameo and pan-India press raise her profile, but core fandom is Hindi-market.
Pan-India face; she actually began in Kannada/started south-rooted, and appeared opposite Rajinikanth in Kochadaiiyaan, so TN recognition is genuine.
Bollywood action-dance icon; widely known by face, but fandom intensity is Hindi-centric.
Prolific Bollywood star; the patriotic/comedy brand is a Hindi-market staple and lands less in TN.
Bollywood action mainstay; Singham (itself a Tamil remake of Singam) gives a thread of TN awareness, but he's a Hindi-belt star.
Suriya's Tamil 'Singam' is the original that Ajay Devgn's Hindi 'Singham' remade; TN viewers see the Hindi cop-franchise as the copy, inverting the usual asymmetry.
Bollywood royalty; 'Poo' from K3G and Jab We Met are Hindi-meme territory, less resonant in Tamil pop culture.
Global-Bollywood crossover; Hollywood fame keeps her known everywhere, but her Hindi-film legacy is the core reference.
Bollywood A-list heroine; widely recognizable, fandom concentrated in Hindi market.
The definitive Hindi-cinema classic; its dialogues are Hindi-belt scripture. Tamil viewers know it exists but don't quote it reflexively.
Sholay's villain is shorthand for menace in Hindi; the name and snack-ad afterlife mean nothing without that Hindi-film context.
Gabbar's iconic Sholay line; a Hindi cultural touchstone that is almost untranslatable as a reference for a Tamil audience.
The archetypal Hindi romance; the Maratha Mandir endless-run and 'Raj' nostalgia are deeply Hindi-market phenomena.
Rare Bollywood film with genuine pan-India penetration; 'All is well' and 'Aal izz well' crossed linguistic lines.
Ranbir Kapoor blockbuster; massive Hindi-market and meme footprint, moderate awareness in TN where local mass films dominate.
Villain Mogambo's catchphrase from Mr. India; pure Hindi-cinema canon, opaque to a Tamil audience.
Cult Hindi sci-fi/masala film; its references (Mogambo, invisibility) are Hindi-belt nostalgia with little TN resonance.
Quintessential Karan Johar family melodrama; a Hindi-NRI-cinema landmark that doesn't register strongly in Tamil Nadu.
Oscar-nominated Aamir Khan film; period-cricket epic widely respected, with moderate TN recall via Aamir's broad appeal.
Aamir Khan wrestling drama; huge globally (esp. China), good TN awareness though the Haryanvi setting is culturally Hindi-north.
Cult Anurag Kashyap crime saga; its meme-able dialogues ('Keh ke lunga') are a Hindi-internet phenomenon, niche in TN.
Bollywood director-producer and chat-show host; the Koffee With Karan ecosystem is intensely Hindi-film-industry inside-baseball.
Opulent Hindi-cinema auteur (Padmaavat, Bajirao); recognized for grandeur, moderate TN footprint.
The flagship Hindi-cinema studio/dynasty; its romance-and-spy-universe brand identity is Hindi-market shorthand.
Known pan-India, but the godlike fan rituals and 'Thalaivar' honorific only fully land in Tamil Nadu.
Revered as Tamil cinema's most versatile actor and now a politician/Bigg Boss host; Hindi audiences mostly know him via older films like Sadma/Hindustani.
Among TN's biggest mass heroes with a political party now; Hindi awareness is medium and rising via dubbed hits like Master/Leo.
Co-leads TN's biggest star rivalry with Vijay; the 'Thala' honorific and his low-key persona are deeply Tamil and barely register in the Hindi belt.
Major Tamil star; Hindi awareness grew sharply with the OTT-released Jai Bhim (2021), but core fandom is TN.
National-award-winning Tamil actor; Hindi audiences know him chiefly via the 'Why This Kolaveri Di' song and Raanjhanaa/The Gray Man.
Acclaimed Tamil method actor (Anniyan, Pithamagan); strong in TN, only medium Hindi recall and easily confused with the 2022 film named 'Vikram'.
Hugely popular Tamil comedy-to-mass hero who rose from TV; almost unknown to Hindi audiences, a sharp asymmetry.
TN's top heroine with the 'Lady Superstar' title; Hindi recall rose via Jawan and the Netflix wedding documentary, but the honorific is purely Tamil.
Long-reigning Tamil/Telugu heroine; iconic in the south but with little Hindi-market presence.
Beloved Tamil actor nicknamed 'Makkal Selvan' (people's treasure); Hindi awareness came via Vikram Vedha remake and the Farzi web series.
Popular Tamil hero (Suriya's brother), strong with TN audiences, minimal Hindi footprint.
Tamil mass-and-music star with a devoted fan base; essentially unknown in the Hindi market.
Landmark Tamil auteur (Nayakan, Roja, Ponniyin Selvan); Hindi cinephiles revere him but mainstream Hindi recall is moderate.
Tamil maximalist director of vigilante blockbusters and Enthiran; widely felt in TN, medium Hindi awareness via dubbed Robot.
Critically lauded Tamil realist director (Asuran, Visaranai, Vada Chennai); strong in TN, niche but respected among Hindi film buffs.
Tamil director behind the interconnected LCU (Kaithi, Vikram, Leo); Hindi awareness rising fast via OTT but the universe lore is TN-fan territory.
Interlinked Tamil action franchise (Kaithi-Vikram-Leo); the shared-universe Easter eggs are devoured by TN fans and largely opaque elsewhere.
Oscar-winning composer known everywhere; he scored both Hindi (Rockstar, Dil Se) and Tamil landmarks, so genuinely shared, with Tamil claiming him as native son.
Towering Tamil composer with thousands of songs; revered as a demigod in TN, only medium recall in the Hindi market.
Chart-dominating young Tamil composer (Kolaveri Di, Leo, Jailer); Hindi awareness growing via viral songs and Jawan, but stardom is TN-scale.
Cult Tamil composer (Ilaiyaraaja's son) with a fierce TN following; virtually unknown in the Hindi market.
Popular 2000s-era Tamil melody composer; beloved nostalgia in TN, no Hindi footprint.
Definitive Rajinikanth mass film; its 'Naan oru thadava sonna' dialogue is TN folklore but means nothing to Hindi audiences.
Rajinikanth-Shankar sci-fi spectacle; the Hindi-dubbed 'Robot' gave it rare pan-India reach, so genuinely shared.
Lokesh's Kamal Haasan action film that launched the LCU; huge in TN, medium Hindi awareness via OTT/dub.
Vijay-Lokesh blockbuster; massive TN event film, medium Hindi recognition through dubbed streaming release.
Vijay vs Vijay Sethupathi hit; the 'Vaathi coming' song crossed over, but the film is a TN phenomenon.
Rajinikanth comeback blockbuster; the 'Kaavaalaa' song went viral nationally, lifting Hindi awareness above its usual Tamil-only level.
Karthi-led single-night thriller that anchors the LCU; revered by TN cinephiles, little Hindi mainstream recall (Hindi remake 'Bholaa' was a flop).
Mani Ratnam's Chola-era epic from the beloved Kalki novel; a cultural event in TN, medium Hindi awareness as a prestige south film.
Dhanush's Tanglish song that became India's first viral music phenomenon; one of the few Tamil-rooted references genuinely known across the Hindi belt.
Anirudh dance anthem from Master; huge TN reel/wedding song with notable Hindi crossover, though the lyric meaning stays Tamil.
The fan ritual of catching the very first screening; an organized, near-religious institution for TN star films, more casual in the Hindi market.
Pouring milk over giant Rajini/Vijay cutouts on release day; an unmistakably Tamil fan-worship ritual that baffles Hindi audiences.
Organized Tamil star fan associations that do social work and mobilize for releases; this structured fandom has no real Hindi-belt equivalent.
Telugu star turned pan-India face via Baahubali; well known in both markets, though originally a south icon.
The Telugu epic that proved pan-India dubbing; 'Why did Kattappa kill Baahubali?' was a national cliffhanger known equally in both regions.
The Baahubali cliffhanger question that gripped all of India; a rare reference with truly symmetric high familiarity across Hindi and Tamil.
Telugu star catapulted to all-India fame by Pushpa; the 'Jhukega nahi' line and beard-stroke gesture became national memes.
Telugu blockbuster whose Hindi dub was a phenomenon; 'Srivalli' and 'Jhukega nahi' are bigger in the Hindi market than the Tamil one.
Rajamouli's Telugu epic that won an Oscar for 'Naatu Naatu'; global and pan-India fame make it broadly familiar in both regions.
The Telugu director behind Baahubali and RRR; his pan-India spectacle brand made his name recognizable far beyond the south.
Oscar-winning RRR dance number; globally and nationally famous, so familiar in both markets despite Telugu origin.
Telugu superstar; RRR boosted his Hindi profile, but as a Telugu (not Tamil) icon he's medium in both reference regions here.
Kannada blockbuster whose dubbed versions were massive everywhere; 'Rocky Bhai' and the Hindi dub gave it especially strong Hindi-belt traction.
Kannada star made pan-India by KGF; his Hindi fame outpaces his Tamil-market recall, which is moderate.
Kannada folk-horror sleeper hit; the Hindi dub made it a national talking point, while TN awareness is more modest.
India's longest-running Hindi sitcom set in a Gujarati-Hindi society; a Hindi-belt daily ritual with almost no Tamil viewership.
The breakout TMKOC character and an endless Hindi meme source; the humor and 'Babita ji' running gags don't travel to Tamil audiences.
Salman-hosted Hindi reality juggernaut; Tamil audiences know the format but follow their own Tamil version more closely.
The Tamil version hosted by Kamal Haasan on Vijay TV; a major TN cultural event each season, essentially unknown to the Hindi audience.

reference: Salman Khan as Bigg Boss host
note: Salman's weekend Bigg Boss hosting is a Hindi-TV institution; the persona is a Hindi-market draw more than a Tamil one.
The dominant Tamil family-entertainment channel and Pongal-premiere hub; central to TN homes, basically off the radar in the Hindi belt.
Leading Tamil channel home to Bigg Boss Tamil and youth shows; a TN staple unknown to Hindi viewers.
Marathon Tamil TV soaps on Sun/Vijay TV; a daily fixture in TN households with no Hindi-audience awareness.
Top Hindi comedy talk show; the Punjabi-Hindi humor and Bollywood-promo format are Hindi-market centric.
Hindi-TV comedy king; well known by name, but his comedic register is rooted in Hindi/Punjabi sensibility.
Amitabh-hosted Hindi quiz show; iconic in the Hindi belt, with a separate Tamil version reducing reliance on the original.
The era-defining Hindi mythological serial; emptied Hindi-belt streets on Sundays, with more moderate TN nostalgia.
Long-running Hindi crime serial and meme factory ('Daya, darwaza tod do'); a Hindi-TV staple with little Tamil following.
Hindi crime-reenactment show; a Hindi-belt television fixture, low TN recall.
Global streamer with strong libraries in both languages; broadly familiar, though originals skew Hindi while Tamil licensing has grown.
Major streamer that invested heavily in both Hindi and Tamil originals/films; well known in both markets.
Mass-reach streamer (cricket + serials in many languages); strong in both, with deep Hindi and Tamil catalogues.
The Sun Network's south-Indian OTT app; a go-to for Tamil films/serials, near-zero Hindi-market presence.
South-focused OTT (Telugu/Tamil) with a dedicated Tamil vertical; relevant to TN viewers, unknown to the Hindi audience.
Streamer strongest in Hindi originals but with Tamil content too; better known in the Hindi market.
India's first Netflix original, a gritty Mumbai crime drama; a Hindi-OTT landmark, watched but less central in TN.
Beloved Prime gentle-comedy set in a UP village; its Hindi-heartland texture is the whole point, so it lands hardest in the Hindi belt.
Prime's UP-gangster series with quotable 'Kaleen Bhaiya' dialogues; a Hindi-OTT phenomenon, moderate TN awareness.
Acclaimed Hindi web series; 'Risk hai toh ishq hai' became a Hindi-market catchphrase, with respectable but smaller TN reach.
Prime spy series whose Season 2 was set in Tamil Nadu with Samantha; that Tamil storyline and casting genuinely boosted its TN relevance.
Tamil/Telugu star; Hindi awareness jumped via The Family Man 2 and the 'Oo Antava' song, but core stardom is in the south.
Tamil-cinema term for a hero's signature swagger line meant to trigger whistles; the concept and the word are TN-fan vocabulary.
South-cinema slang for crowd-pleasing hero elevation; deeply embedded in Tamil fan talk, only loosely understood in the Hindi market.
The high-impact pre-intermission scene; a celebrated craft beat in Tamil cinema discourse, less of a named concept in the Hindi belt.
The elaborate slow-mo star-introduction beat that TN fans whistle for; a hallmark of Tamil mass films with little named-concept status in Hindi cinema.
Means 'commander', the fan title for Vijay; the word and its weight are purely Tamil and opaque to Hindi audiences.
Means 'leader/head', the fan title for Ajith; recognizable only within Tamil star culture.
Tamil rallying cry ('blow the whistle') for theatre celebration, also a CSK cricket slogan; an unmistakably TN expression.
Tamil comedian whose film expressions power an entire TN meme template economy; the reaction-GIF culture is incomprehensible without Tamil context.
Legendary Tamil comedy duo of the 80s-90s; nostalgic TN reference points absent from Hindi pop culture.
Iconic Hindi-cinema comedian; a Bollywood-comedy fixture whose bits are Hindi-market nostalgia.
The Hindi Munna Bhai films gave 'Gandhigiri' and 'jadoo ki jhappi' to the Hindi lexicon; moderate TN recall.
Controversial Hindi remake of Telugu Arjun Reddy; a Hindi-market talking point, with TN viewers more attached to the original.
The Hindi Stree-Bhediya horror-comedy franchise rooted in North-Indian folklore; its 'O Stree kal aana' gag is Hindi-belt specific.
SRK blockbuster directed by Tamil filmmaker Atlee with Nayanthara and Anirudh's music; that Tamil-talent crossover makes it genuinely shared.
Tamil director (Theri, Mersal, Bigil) who broke into Bollywood with Jawan; established in TN, rising Hindi recognition.
Inventive Tamil director (Jigarthanda, Petta); a TN-cinephile favorite with little Hindi-market profile.
Tamil director known for anti-caste, Ambedkarite cinema (Kaala, Sarpatta); his sociopolitical lens is deeply TN-rooted and niche elsewhere.
The TN tradition of major star films releasing for Pongal (mid-Jan); a Tamil festival-cinema calendar with no Hindi-belt parallel.
Bollywood's habit of slotting tentpoles to Diwali and Eid (esp. Salman's Eid films); a Hindi-market release rhythm.
Rajini's signature one-liners and cigarette-flip swagger; iconic and quotable in TN, only loosely known to Hindi audiences via clips.
